trials.reason: Book B introduces the core EntryQueryHelpers implementation and its unit tests before touching the consuming FwDataMiniLcmApi and integration/snapshot tests, matching definitions-before-uses and building toward the change's payoff.

Book B introduces the core EntryQueryHelpers definitions and their unit tests before showing consuming/behavioral changes (FwDataMiniLcmApi, sync tests, snapshots), whereas Book A leads with call-site tweaks and integration tests before the underlying definitions.

Book A introduces the core EntryQueryHelpers/LcmCrdtKernel implementation and its dedicated unit tests before the downstream consumer changes (FwDataMiniLcmApi, cross-implementation tests, sync-test exclusions), so definitions precede uses, whereas Book B leads with consumer-side tweaks before the underlying implementation is shown.
